sentences.sentence: The problem of how health-care resources should be allocated or apportioned, so that they are distributed in both the most just and most efficient way, is not a new one.
sentences.translation: 如何分配医疗资源，使其以最公正、最高效的方式分布，并不是新问题。

structure.text: The problem of how health-care resources should be allocated or apportioned
so that they are distributed in both the most just and most efficient way
is not a new one

difficult_points: how 从句作 problem 的内容，so that 表示分配目标。
allocated 和 apportioned 是同义并列。

sentences.sentence: Every health system in an economically developed society is faced with the need to decide (either formally or informally) what proportion of the community's total resources should be spent on health-care; how resources are to be apportioned; what diseases and disabilities and which forms of treatment are to be given priority; which members of the community are to be given special consideration in respect of their health needs; and which forms of treatment are the most cost-effective.
sentences.translation: 经济发达社会的每个卫生系统都必须决定：社区总资源中有多少应花在医疗上；资源如何分配；哪些疾病、残疾和治疗方式优先；哪些成员的健康需求应获特别考虑；哪些治疗方式最具成本效益。

structure.text: Every health system in an economically developed society
is faced with the need to decide

what proportion of the community's total resources should be spent on health-care; how resources are to be apportioned; what diseases and disabilities and which forms of treatment are to be given priority; which members of the community are to be given special consideration in respect of their health needs; and which forms of treatment are the most cost-effective

difficult_points: need to decide 后列出五项并列决策内容。
be to be given 是表示安排或要求的被动结构。

expressions.text: be faced with the need to

sentences.sentence: What is new is that, from the 1950s onwards, there have been certain general changes in outlook about the finitude of resources as a whole and of health-care resources in particular, as well as more specific changes regarding the clientele of health-care resources and the cost to the community of those resources.
sentences.translation: 新的是，自20世纪50年代起，人们对总体资源尤其是医疗资源的有限性，以及医疗资源服务对象和社会成本，产生了普遍和具体的观念变化。

structure.text: What is new
is that, from the 1950s onwards, there have been certain general changes in outlook about the finitude of resources as a whole and of health-care resources in particular
from the 1950s onwards
as well as more specific changes regarding the clientele of health-care resources and the cost to the community of those resources

difficult_points: What is new 作主语，is that 引出新变化。
as well as 连接两类变化，finitude 表示有限性。

sentences.sentence: Thus, in the 1950s and 1960s, there emerged an awareness in Western societies that resources for the provision of fossil fuel energy were finite and exhaustible and that the capacity of nature or the environment to sustain economic development and population was also finite.
sentences.translation: 因此，20世纪50、60年代，西方社会意识到，提供化石燃料能源的资源是有限且会耗尽的，自然或环境维持经济发展和人口的能力也有限。

structure.text: in the 1950s and 1960s
there emerged an awareness in Western societies
that resources for the provision of fossil fuel energy were finite and exhaustible and that the capacity of nature or the environment to sustain economic development and population was also finite

difficult_points: there emerged 是倒装存在句。
两个 that 从句并列说明 awareness 内容，to sustain 修饰 capacity。

sentences.sentence: In other words, we became aware of the obvious fact that there were "limits to growth".
sentences.translation: 换句话说，我们意识到一个显而易见的事实：增长是有“极限”的。

structure.text: In other words
we became aware of the obvious fact
that there were "limits to growth"

difficult_points: that 从句同位解释 fact。
limits to growth 是固定概念。

sentences.sentence: The new consciousness that there were also severe limits to health-care resources was part of this general revelation of the obvious.
sentences.translation: 意识到医疗资源也有严重限制，是这一普遍显而易见发现的一部分。

structure.text: The new consciousness that there were also severe limits to health-care resources
was part of this general revelation of the obvious

difficult_points: that 从句修饰 consciousness，说明意识内容。
revelation of the obvious 指认识到显而易见的事实。

sentences.sentence: Looking back, it now seems quite incredible that in the national health systems that emerged in many countries in the years immediately after the 1939-45 World War, it was assumed without question that all the basic health needs of any community could be satisfied, at least in principle; the "invisible hand" of economic progress would provide.
sentences.translation: 回头看，令人难以置信的是，二战后许多国家建立的卫生系统曾毫无疑问地假定，至少原则上任何社区的基本健康需求都能得到满足；经济进步这只“看不见的手”会提供一切。

structure.text: Looking back
it now seems quite incredible

that in the national health systems that emerged in many countries in the years immediately after the 1939-45 World War, it was assumed without question that all the basic health needs of any community could be satisfied, at least in principle
that emerged in many countries in the years immediately after the 1939-45 World War

difficult_points: it 是形式主语，外层 that 从句是真正内容。
内层 it was assumed 后又嵌套 that 从句；分号后是经济进步的比喻。

sentences.sentence: However, at exactly the same time as this new realization of the finite character of health-care resources was sinking in, an awareness of a contrary kind was developing in Western societies: that people have a basic right to health-care as a necessary condition of a proper human life.
sentences.translation: 然而，正当人们逐渐认识到医疗资源有限时，西方社会也形成了相反的意识：人们拥有医疗权，这是正常人类生活的必要条件。

structure.text: at exactly the same time as this new realization of the finite character of health-care resources was sinking in
an awareness of a contrary kind was developing in Western societies
that people have a basic right to health-care as a necessary condition of a proper human life

difficult_points: at the same time as 引导时间从句，sinking in 表示逐渐被理解。
冒号后的 that 从句解释相反意识。

sentences.sentence: Like education, political and legal processes and institutions, public order, communication, transport and money supply, health-care came to be seen as one of the fundamental social facilities necessary for people to exercise their other rights as autonomous human beings.
sentences.translation: 和教育、政治法律制度、公共秩序、通信、交通及货币供给一样，医疗被视为人们作为自主个体行使其他权利所必需的基本社会设施之一。

structure.text: Like education, political and legal processes and institutions, public order, communication, transport and money supply
health-care came to be seen as one of the fundamental social facilities
necessary for people to exercise their other rights as autonomous human beings

difficult_points: Like 后长列举构成比较背景。
necessary for... 修饰 facilities，to exercise 表示目的。

sentences.sentence: People are not in a position to exercise personal liberty and to be self-determining if they are poverty-stricken, or deprived of basic education, or do not live within a context of law and order.
sentences.translation: 如果人们贫困、被剥夺基本教育，或生活在无法律秩序的环境中，他们就无法行使个人自由、实现自我决定。

structure.text: People are not in a position to exercise personal liberty and to be self-determining
if they are poverty-stricken, or deprived of basic education, or do not live within a context of law and order

difficult_points: if 从句列出三种阻碍条件。
to exercise 与 to be self-determining 并列作 position 的补足。

sentences.sentence: In the same way, basic health-care is a condition of the exercise of autonomy.
sentences.translation: 同样，基本医疗是行使自主权的条件。

structure.text: In the same way
basic health-care
is a condition of the exercise of autonomy

difficult_points: In the same way 承接上一句逻辑。
condition of 后接名词 exercise，说明医疗的基础作用。

sentences.sentence: Although the language of "rights" sometimes leads to confusion, by the late 1970s it was recognized in most societies that people have a right to health-care (though there has been considerable resistance in the United States to the idea that there is a formal right to health-care).
sentences.translation: 尽管“权利”一词有时令人困惑，但到20世纪70年代末，大多数社会已承认人们拥有医疗权（尽管美国一直强烈抵制正式医疗权的观念）。

structure.text: Although the language of "rights" sometimes leads to confusion
by the late 1970s it was recognized in most societies
that people have a right to health-care
though there has been considerable resistance in the United States to the idea that there is a formal right to health-care

difficult_points: Although 和 though 分别引导外层及括号内让步。
it was recognized that 是形式主语结构。

sentences.sentence: It is also accepted that this right generates an obligation or duty for the state to ensure that adequate health-care resources are provided out of the public purse.
sentences.translation: 人们也接受这样的观点：这项权利产生了国家的义务，即确保由公共资金提供充足的医疗资源。

structure.text: It is also accepted
that this right generates an obligation or duty for the state
to ensure that adequate health-care resources are provided out of the public purse
that adequate health-care resources are provided out of the public purse

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，that 从句为内容。
to ensure 后再嵌套 that 被动从句，out of the public purse 表示公共财政。

sentences.sentence: The state has no obligation to provide a health-care system itself, but to ensure that such a system is provided.
sentences.translation: 国家没有义务亲自提供医疗系统，而是有义务确保这样的系统得到提供。

structure.text: The state has no obligation to provide a health-care system itself
but to ensure that such a system is provided

difficult_points: not...but... 对比国家不必亲自提供、但必须确保提供。
that 从句作 ensure 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: Put another way, basic health-care is now recognized as a "public good", rather than a "private good" that one is expected to buy for oneself.
sentences.translation: 换句话说，基本医疗如今被视为“公共物品”，而不是人们应自行购买的“私人物品”。

structure.text: Put another way
basic health-care is now recognized as a "public good"
rather than a "private good" that one is expected to buy for oneself
that one is expected to buy for oneself

difficult_points: Put another way 是换述表达。
rather than 对比 public good 和 private good；that 修饰 private good。

sentences.sentence: As the 1976 declaration of the World Health Organization put it: "The enjoyment of the highest attainable standard of health is one of the fundamental rights of every human being without distinction of race, religion, political belief, economic or social condition".
sentences.translation: 正如世界卫生组织1976年宣言所说：“享有可达到的最高健康标准是每个人的基本权利，不因种族、宗教、政治信仰、经济或社会状况而有区别。”

structure.text: As the 1976 declaration of the World Health Organization put it
The enjoyment of the highest attainable standard of health is one of the fundamental rights of every human being
without distinction of race, religion, political belief, economic or social condition

difficult_points: As...put it 引出原文引语。
without distinction of 列举不应影响权利的身份条件。

expressions.text: the highest attainable standard of health
expressions.meaning_zh: 可达到的最高健康标准

sentences.sentence: As has just been remarked, in a liberal society basic health is seen as one of the indispensable conditions for the exercise of personal autonomy.
sentences.translation: 正如刚才所说，在自由社会中，基本健康被视为行使个人自主权不可或缺的条件之一。

structure.text: As has just been remarked
in a liberal society
basic health is seen as one of the indispensable conditions for the exercise of personal autonomy

difficult_points: As has been remarked 是被动省略结构。
be seen as 后接 one of conditions，for 表示用途。

sentences.sentence: Just at the time when it became obvious that health-care resources could not possibly meet the demands being made upon them, people were demanding that their fundamental right to health-care be satisfied by the state.
sentences.translation: 就在医疗资源显然不可能满足对其提出的需求时，人们要求国家满足他们享有医疗的基本权利。

structure.text: Just at the time when it became obvious that health-care resources could not possibly meet the demands being made upon them
people were demanding
that their fundamental right to health-care be satisfied by the state
being made upon them

difficult_points: when 从句中又嵌套 that 从句。
demand 后接虚拟语气 be satisfied，表示要求。

sentences.sentence: The second set of more specific changes that have led to the present concern about the distribution of health-care resources stems from the dramatic rise in health costs in most OECD countries, accompanied by large-scale demographic and social changes which have meant, to take one example, that elderly people are now major (and relatively very expensive) consumers of health-care resources.
sentences.translation: 导致当下关注医疗资源分配的第二组具体变化，源于多数经合组织国家医疗成本的急剧上升，并伴随大规模人口和社会变化，例如老年人如今成为医疗资源的主要且相对昂贵的消费者。

structure.text: The second set of more specific changes that have led to the present concern about the distribution of health-care resources
stems from the dramatic rise in health costs in most OECD countries
accompanied by large-scale demographic and social changes
which have meant, to take one example, that elderly people are now major (and relatively very expensive) consumers of health-care resources

difficult_points: that have led 修饰 changes；stems from 表示来源。
which 从句中又嵌套 that，to take one example 插入举例。

sentences.sentence: Thus in OECD countries as a whole, health costs increased from 3.8% of GDP in 1960 to 7% of GDP in 1980, and it has been predicted that the proportion of health costs to GDP will continue to increase.
sentences.translation: 因此，在经合组织国家整体中，医疗成本占GDP的比例从1960年的3.8%升至1980年的7%，并且预计医疗成本占GDP的比例还会继续上升。

structure.text: in OECD countries as a whole
health costs increased from 3.8% of GDP in 1960 to 7% of GDP in 1980
it has been predicted that the proportion of health costs to GDP will continue to increase

difficult_points: from...to... 表示比例变化。
it has been predicted that 是形式主语被动结构。

sentences.sentence: (In the US the current figure is about 12% of GDP, and in Australia about 7.8% of GDP.) As a consequence, during the 1980s a kind of doomsday scenario (analogous to similar doomsday extrapolations about energy needs and fossil fuels or about population increases) was projected by health administrators, economists and politicians.
sentences.translation: （美国目前约为GDP的12%，澳大利亚约为7.8%。）因此，20世纪80年代，卫生管理者、经济学家和政治家提出了一种末日情景，类似于对能源、化石燃料需求或人口增长的悲观推算。

structure.text: In the US the current figure is about 12% of GDP, and in Australia about 7.8% of GDP

during the 1980s a kind of doomsday scenario (analogous to similar doomsday extrapolations about energy needs and fossil fuels or about population increases) was projected by health administrators, economists and politicians
analogous to similar doomsday extrapolations about energy needs and fossil fuels or about population increases

difficult_points: 括号提供美国和澳大利亚数据，主句用被动 was projected。
analogous to 短语说明末日情景的类比来源。

sentences.sentence: In this scenario, ever-rising health costs were matched against static or declining resources.
sentences.translation: 在这一情景中，不断上升的医疗成本与停滞或下降的资源相对照。

structure.text: In this scenario
ever-rising health costs
were matched against static or declining resources

difficult_points: ever-rising 表示持续上升。
be matched against 表示拿来与……比较或对照。
